title: skills: genericize handoff-templates + housekeeping attributions
description:
## Context
handoff-templates.md and housekeeping/SKILL.md are distributed reference
files (they ship to the team via /distribute). They carried a personal
attribution ("Luca Ronin") and LinearB-fleet specifics (Asana / "GTM
Engineering project"), plus a now-dangling /asana cross-reference.
Generic harness infrastructure should not name specific people or
internal projects. Surfaced 2026-05-21 during the verbosity-pass review.

## Task
Strip the personal ADR attribution from both files; genericize the
receipt-of-work pattern in handoff-templates.md (drop Asana/GTM
specifics and the dead /asana cross-ref; keep the generic "a receipt is
not a bead type" lesson); make the eval path a <project> placeholder.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No personal-name or LinearB-internal references remain
- [ ] No instructional content lost — only attribution/specificity removed
- [ ] Adversarial review (/scrutinize) verdict: SHIP
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] handoff-templates.md + housekeeping genericized
 - [ ] no instructional content lost
 - [ ] /scrutinize verdict SHIP

title: onboard: promote skill-loading to its own gated step
description:
## Context
When a user invokes /onboard, the agent unreliably reads all skill
bodies — it skips some, or delegates the read to an Explore agent whose
context is then discarded. The user has had to explicitly say "read all
skills directly, no Explore agent" every session. The skill already
said to do this, but as buried item 4 of Step 1, framed as an outcome
rather than a forcing procedure.

## Task
Promote skill-loading to its own numbered step (Step 2): an
enumerate-via-ls read list, an explicit forbidden-shortcuts list
(Explore-agent delegation / sampling / skip-because-long), and a
verification gate — the orientation report must state "Skills loaded:
N/N". Renumber subsequent steps and fix cross-references.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Skill-loading is its own step with a concrete ls procedure
- [ ] Forbidden shortcuts named explicitly
- [ ] Orientation report gates on the N/N count
- [ ] Step renumbering + cross-refs consistent
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] skill-loading is its own step with an ls procedure
 - [ ] forbidden shortcuts named; report gates on N/N count
 - [ ] step renumbering + cross-refs consistent

title: skills: verbosity trim pass per the 2026-05-21 audit
description:
## Context
The 2026-05-21 4-agent skill audit flagged most technical how-to skills
as 15-35% trimmable. The biggest wins were already absorbed by the
2026-05-21 restructure: /asana rewritten lean (~85 lines), /openrouter's
54-line personal section cut, /gdoc's connector-dump extracted, /duckdb
retired entirely. This bead tracks the RESIDUAL trims — pure polish, no
defects; the skills are committed and functional.

## Task — trim per the audit's specific findings
- gamma (~25-30%): the 3 Style A/B/C JSONC payloads duplicate
  reference/3-styles.md + reference/example-deterministic.json — keep
  one inline, reference the rest; "Common workflows" and "Guardrails"
  restate the "CRITICAL: cost awareness" section — consolidate.
- beads (~20%): the bv section duplicates reference/bv.md (command
  table + jq recipes); the ADR-conventions block duplicates
  reference/handoff-templates.md. KEEP "Choosing a type" + "Stages vs
  gates" in the body — the user explicitly wants them there.
- nginx (~10%): the certbot-managed-vhost gotcha is stated twice in
  full — collapse the second to a back-reference.
- lint (~5-10%, minor): "Real-world recovery patterns" overlaps the
  "When to disable a rule" decision tree.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Each skill trimmed per the findings; no loss of load-bearing content
- [ ] /distribute run afterward so the team copy stays current

title: hooks: wire the .offboard-pending marker (documented but never created)
description:
## Problem
/offboard and /onboard both document a `.offboard-pending` marker:
/offboard claims pre-compact.sh + session-end.sh create it, and
/onboard Step 0 checks for it to run /offboard retroactively. No hook
actually creates the file — session-end.sh only flushes bead state.

## Symptom
The retroactive-offboard safety net is dead code. A session that ends
without /offboard is never caught by the next session's /onboard,
because the marker /onboard checks for is never written.

## Cause
session-end.sh was never given marker-creation logic; the skill docs
describe a mechanism that was never implemented.

## Source
Surfaced 2026-05-21 by the harness hooks-analysis review.

## Fix
Wire session-end.sh to drop .offboard-pending at the repo root unless
HEAD committed this session's handoff note (= /offboard ran). Keep
pre-compact.sh out of it — a same-session post-compaction /onboard
would misread its own marker. Correct the /offboard + /onboard docs;
gitignore the marker.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] session-end.sh creates .offboard-pending when /offboard didn't run
- [ ] no marker when /offboard ran (HEAD committed the handoff note)
- [ ] skipped inside worktrees
- [ ] /offboard + /onboard docs corrected; .offboard-pending gitignored
- [ ] regression test test-session-end.sh passes
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] session-end.sh wires the marker correctly (offboard-ran detection + worktree skip)
 - [ ] /offboard + /onboard docs corrected; marker gitignored
 - [ ] test-session-end.sh passes
